input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Search Jan 8 articles for key topics
topics = {
    'Brisbane tennis': ['Brisbane', 'Sabalenka'],
    'AFCON QF': ['AFCON', 'quarterfinal', 'Morocco', 'Algeria', 'Nigeria'],
    'Iran province': ['Iran', 'police', 'killed'],
    'Turkey Grok': ['Grok', 'ban'],
    'Somalia': ['Somalia'],
    'BRICS navy': ['BRICS', 'Ramaphosa'],
    'Senate budget': ['Senate', 'budget', 'funding'],
    'Man United': ['Manchester United', 'Fletcher', 'interim'],
    'Atletico Madrid': ['Atletico', 'Copa del Rey'],
}
counts = {}
with open('articles/2026/01/08/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = a.get('title','') or ''
        for topic, kws in topics.items():
            for kw in kws:
                if kw.lower() in title.lower():
                    counts[topic] = counts.get(topic, 0) + 1
                    break
for topic, count in sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f'{topic}: {count}')
"

python3 -c "
import json
# Search for Grok ban/block articles across all recent dates
for date_dir in ['2026/01/06', '2026/01/07', '2026/01/08']:
    with open(f'articles/{date_dir}/articles.jsonl') as f:
        for line in f:
            a = json.loads(line)
            title = a.get('title','') or ''
            content = a.get('content','') or ''
            if 'Grok' in title and ('ban' in content.lower()[:2000] or 'block' in content.lower()[:2000] or 'Turkey' in content[:2000] or 'India' in content[:2000] or 'Malaysia' in content[:2000]):
                print(f'{date_dir}: {title[:120]}')
" | head -10
